cards.title: Price Anchoring
cards.body: Customers perceive a product as cheaper when compared to a more expensive option, even if they have no intention of buying the pricier item.

cards.title: The 60-30-10 Rule
cards.body: In sales, 60% of customers are not a good fit, 30% are a potential fit, and 10% are a perfect fit.

cards.title: Priming
cards.body: Exposing customers to certain words or images can influence their purchasing decisions, making them more likely to buy.

cards.title: The Baader-Meinhof Phenomenon
cards.body: When people notice a product or brand, they are more likely to notice it again, making them think it's more popular than it is.

cards.title: Decoy Pricing
cards.body: A higher-priced option can make a similarly-priced option seem like a better value, increasing sales.

cards.title: Limited-Time Offers
cards.body: Creating a sense of urgency with limited-time offers can increase sales by 20-30%.

cards.title: Charity-Linked Sales
cards.body: Products linked to charitable causes can increase sales by 10-15%, as customers feel good about supporting a good cause.

cards.title: The Endowment Effect
cards.body: People place a higher value on things they already own, making them more likely to keep rather than sell or trade.

cards.title: Bundling and Unbundling
cards.body: Offering services or products separately or together can increase overall sales and customer satisfaction.

cards.title: Post-Purchase Rationalization
cards.body: After making a purchase, customers often rationalize their decision, telling themselves it was a good choice, even if it wasn't.

quizzes.question: What phenomenon makes people notice a product more after initial exposure?
quizzes.explanation: The Baader-Meinhof Phenomenon explains how people notice a product more after initial exposure.

quizzes.question: What tactic can increase sales by creating a sense of urgency?
quizzes.explanation: Limited-time offers create a sense of urgency, increasing sales.

quizzes.question: What is the approximate distribution of customer fit in sales?
quizzes.explanation: The 60-30-10 rule states that 60% of customers are not a good fit, 30% are a potential fit, and 10% are a perfect fit.

quizzes.question: What effect makes people value things they already own more?
quizzes.explanation: The Endowment Effect explains how people value things they already own more.

quizzes.question: What happens to customers after making a purchase?
options: They start to question their decision
They become indifferent to their decision
quizzes.explanation: After making a purchase, customers often rationalize their decision.

guesses.prompt: By what percentage can limited-time offers increase sales?
guesses.explanation: Limited-time offers can increase sales due to the created sense of urgency.

guesses.prompt: By what percentage can charity-linked sales increase sales?
guesses.explanation: Charity-linked sales can increase sales as customers feel good about supporting a good cause.

guesses.prompt: What percentage of customers are a perfect fit according to the 60-30-10 rule?
guesses.explanation: The 60-30-10 rule provides insight into customer fit.
